quiz.title: Unit 14 Quiz 3: Parametric Equations
quiz.subtitle: Derivatives, Tangents, Speed, and Arc Length
quiz.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A curve is defined by $x = t^2 - 4$, $y = \ln(t^2 + 1)$. Find $\frac{dy}{dx}$ in terms of $t$.
Find the slope of the tangent line to the curve $x = 3\cos\theta$, $y = 2\sin\theta$ at $\theta = \frac{\pi}{3}$.
For the parametric curve $x = t^3 - 3t$, $y = t^2 - 4$, find the point(s) where the tangent line is horizontal.
questions.explanation: Horizontal tangent when $\frac{dy}{dt}=0$ and $\frac{dx}{dt}\neq0$. $\frac{dy}{dt}=2t=0 \Rightarrow t=0$. Then $x=0$, $y=-4$. $\frac{dx}{dt}=3t^2-3=-3\neq0$, so point is $(0,-4)$.

questions.question: A particle moves with position $x = t^2 - 2t$, $y = t^3 - 6t$. Find the speed of the particle at $t = 1$.
Find $\frac{d^2y}{dx^2}$ for the curve $x = \ln t$, $y = t^2$ at $t = e$.
A particle moves along a path given by $x = 2\cos t$, $y = \sin(2t)$. Find the speed at $t = \frac{\pi}{4}$.
Find the length of the arc of the curve $x = t^3$, $y = t^2$ from $t = 0$ to $t = 1$.
Find an equation of the tangent line to the curve $x = 3t^2$, $y = 2t^3$ at the point where $t = 1$.
For the curve $x = t^3 - 6t$, $y = t^2 - 2t$, find the points where the tangent line is vertical.
questions.explanation: Vertical tangent when $\frac{dx}{dt}=0$ and $\frac{dy}{dt}\neq0$. $\frac{dx}{dt}=3t^2-6=0 \Rightarrow t=\pm\sqrt{2}$. For $t=\sqrt{2}$: $x=-4\sqrt{2}$, $y=2-2\sqrt{2}$; for $t=-\sqrt{2}$: $x=4\sqrt{2}$, $y=2+2\sqrt{2}$. Both have $\frac{dy}{dt}=2t-2\neq0$.

questions.question: A curve is given by $x = 2\sec\theta$, $y = 3\tan\theta$ for $-\frac{\pi}{2} < \theta < \frac{\pi}{2}$. Find $\frac{dy}{dx}$ in simplified form.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered parametric equations.
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review arc length and higher derivatives.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on derivatives of parametric curves.
scoreMessages.0: Review parametric derivatives and tangent lines.
